label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out which of the two options is sarcastic. Let's start by understanding what each option is saying. Option A: "One of the worst things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." This statement is straightforward. It's saying that pushing a trolley of returns is one of the worst tasks in retail, which makes sense because it's a common complaint in retail jobs about the monotonous or undesirable tasks. Option B: "One of the enjoyable things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." This statement is saying that pushing a trolley of returns is an enjoyable task. However, based on general knowledge, pushing a trolley of returns is typically seen as a boring, tedious, and unpleasant task in retail. So, if someone says it's enjoyable when it's actually not, that's likely sarcasm. Sarcasm often involves saying the opposite of what is meant, usually in a mocking or ironic way. In
